I've read that it's normal to feel withdrawal symptoms, and it's not the end of the world if I slip up. But I really want to make it through those 3 or 4 days, and I think reminding myself of why I'm doing this - for the baby and my own health - will help me stay motivated.

Therapist: You've thought a lot about how to handle withdrawal symptoms and stay motivated, and it sounds like reminding yourself of your reasons for quitting will be a big help. How can you make those reasons more concrete and easily accessible to you, especially in the moments when you're feeling a strong craving?

Patient: I think I'll take a picture of my wife's sonogram and put it on my fridge, so every time I walk into the kitchen I'll see it. And maybe I'll even write a little reminder on my phone to look at it whenever I get a craving. That way, it'll be right there in front of me, and I won't be able to ignore it.

One sentence only. Nothing more.
